name: AutoGPTQ
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); requires NVIDIA GPU for quantization; no API costs

performance.notes: GPTQ quantization reduces model memory 2-4x; inference speed similar to BF16 with lower VRAM

agent_readiness.notes: Post-training quantization library for LLMs using the GPTQ algorithm. Quantizes LLMs to 4-bit/3-bit/2-bit precision with minimal quality degradation. Integrates with HuggingFace Transformers and PEFT. Widely used for serving large models (70B+) on consumer GPUs. GPTQ-quantized models available on HuggingFace Hub (thousands of models). Triton kernel backend for faster inference.

gotchas: Quantization requires calibration dataset and NVIDIA GPU — not CPU-only
Quality degradation at 2-bit is significant; 4-bit generally acceptable
Slower to quantize (minutes to hours for large models) vs inference-only bitsandbytes
ExLlamaV2 generally faster for inference of GPTQ models
best_when: Running 70B+ parameter models on consumer GPUs (24GB VRAM)
Distributing quantized versions of fine-tuned models
Reducing inference costs in production via lower VRAM requirements
When quality matters more than speed (GPTQ > bitsandbytes for quality)
summary: AutoGPTQ is a post-training quantization library that applies the GPTQ algorithm to compress LLMs to 4-bit/3-bit precision with minimal quality loss. Enables running large models (70B+) on consumer GPUs with 24GB VRAM. Integrates with HuggingFace Transformers. Thousands of pre-quantized models on HuggingFace Hub. Best for high-quality 4-bit quantization when GPTQ quality matters over simple bitsandbytes loading.
